market_regime.interpretation: Put walls are temporary support (breakdown likely). Call walls are strong resistance. Gamma flip acts as dynamic resistance.

outlook.volatilityExpectation: High volatility expected due to heavily negative total GEX; any move away from spot will be amplified by dealer hedging.
outlook.summary: Market makers hold massive short gamma positions across all expirations, creating an unstable and reactive environment. A heavy Put Wall at 700 serves as a critical structural floor, while immediate Call resistance lies just above at 708-715. Extreme put volume and a bearish volatility skew confirm strong downside hedging, but negative GEX implies that any breach of support could trigger an accelerated sell-off.

levels.motivazione: Massive Put OI across all expirations (0DTE: 15.1k, Weekly: 42k, Monthly: 45.4k) creates a major structural support. Negative GEX makes this a critical dealer defense line.
At-the-money friction point with significant OI in all expirations. Huge 0DTE Put volume (306k) and heavy Monthly Call OI (33k) create a gamma battleground.
Major 0DTE resistance with 10.6k Call OI and massive unusual call volume (161k), indicating strong selling interest and a hard ceiling for the intraday session.
Concentrated Weekly support with 69.2k Put OI, reinforced by Monthly Put OI. Secondary institutional floor below the 700 level.
Deep institutional support with heavy OI in Weekly (61k) and Monthly (81.9k). Ultimate structural floor for the current options series.
Significant Weekly Call OI (22.9k) creates an immediate resistance barrier just above the current spot price, capping upside momentum.
0DTE Max Pain level. With spot currently at 710.34, this acts as an intraday price attractor for market makers to minimize payouts.
Strong Monthly support (46.6k OI) coinciding with the Monthly Max Pain (690.0), acting as a massive gravitational pull lower if 695 is breached.
Weekly Max Pain level. Located below spot, it exerts a downward pull on the price action for the weekly expiration.
